spec_match.rationale: Covers part of the spec (1 of 3 named API surfaces) — distinct angle worth picking up.

interactive.rationale: Real controls + script-driven side-effects make this hands-on, not a static card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Page covers the promises the feature index makes for this concept.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path is covered but only a few failure modes are surfaced.

references.rationale: Cites the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> reference link as required.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex, button overrides keep bg+color paired.

openQuestions.title: Surface more named spec API for math-sumprecise
openQuestions.detail: The spec summary lists API surfaces this concept doesn't currently name on-page. Either rename the controls / readouts to mention them, or add a second concept page that targets the missing surface directly. The reviewer extracted these from the spec: Math.sumPrecise(iterable). Returns the maximally-correct sum of an iterable of numbers (Shewchuk-style adaptive precision). Empty iterable returns -0. Non-numeric inputs throw. BigInt excluded. NaN/Infinity follow IEEE 754. Stage 3 at TC39. Differs from naive reduce((a,b)=>a+b,0) — example [1e20, 0.1, -1e20] returns 0.1 vs 0. Authors must not assume specific algorithm; only the correctness guarant...

openQuestions.title: Cover at least one failure mode
openQuestions.detail: The page currently shows the happy path. Add a UI affordance that drives the feature into a failure mode the spec explicitly calls out (timeout, denied permission, cross-origin block, malformed input) and surface what the browser actually does.

summary: Workable concept with a couple of gaps. 2 open question(s) queued.
